summary: Recent updates to the image decoding capabilities in Firefox include improvements in handling SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics) files by adding link-parameter parsing and rendering. This enhancement allows for more complex interactivity and design within SVG images. Additionally, a fix was implemented for animated GIFs, addressing a specific issue where GIFs with a zero first-frame delay would fail to decode properly. These changes ensure that users have a smoother experience when viewing interactive and animated images on the web, making Firefox more reliable for rich media content. **This means that animated GIFs will now load correctly, enhancing users' enjoyment of animated visuals online.**
